mnemonics.title: Kahn's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: CID: Compute indegrees → Initialize queue with sources → Dequeue, add to result, decrease neighbor indegrees, enqueue if zero

mnemonics.title: DFS Topological Sort
mnemonics.content: FISH: Finish time → In stack push → Sort (pop to get order) → Handle all vertices

mnemonics.title: Cycle Detection in Kahn's
mnemonics.content: Less V Less: Less than V vertices processed indicates Cycle

acronyms.usage: The class of graphs for which topological sorting is possible
Kahn's algorithm uses BFS-like approach with a queue
Alternative topological sort method using finish times

visualTips.visualization: Think of tasks as a timeline from left to right, where arrows always point from earlier to later tasks
Imagine water flowing from sources (indegree 0) downstream, processing nodes as they become accessible
Like post-order traversal: leaves finish first and get added to stack first, so they appear last in output

analogies.analogy: Like planning a trip where you must visit cities in a specific order based on one-way flights - you can only fly from A to B if the flight exists
Like prerequisite courses: you can't take Data Structures before Introduction to Programming - this forms a DAG
If in your todo list, Task A depends on B, B depends on C, and C depends on A - you're stuck in a loop (cycle), can't start anything
